summary: The presence of stretch-sensitive KCNQ1 mutations contributes to altered cardiac repolarization and increased risk for arrhythmias due to impaired electrical stability; these effects interact synergistically with environmental stresses—particularly those causing myocardial stretch—to exacerbate atrial fibrillation pathology through disrupted ion homeostasis and heightened vulnerability to triggered activity. This highlights how genetically determined ion channel dysfunctions can amplify environmentally induced mechanical strain into clinically significant arrhythmic events.
